query: Does giving an LLM a structured negotiation script improve a human's bargaining outcomes (deal value)?

passages.text: In a randomized study of 240 participants, users coached by an LLM that followed a structured BATNA-anchoring script reached agreements with 12% higher joint surplus than an unscripted-chatbot control, and the effect held after controlling for prior negotiation experience.

Pairing novice negotiators with a turn-by-turn LLM advisor that prompted them to state their reservation value before conceding raised their captured deal value by 0.4 standard deviations relative to negotiating alone.
An LLM negotiation advisor significantly improved users' self-reported satisfaction with the negotiation experience, even though it did not change the final deal terms they reached.
Negotiators using a scripted LLM advisor reduced the average time to reach agreement by roughly 20% compared with the unaided control.
Participants who used an LLM negotiation script reported lower anxiety in the minutes before the negotiation began.
Large language models can be prompted to role-play distinct negotiation personas, which is useful for generating training dialogues.
Transformer attention complexity grows quadratically with sequence length, motivating sparse-attention variants for long documents.

query: What sample sizes are typical in studies measuring AI coaching effects on negotiation skill?

passages.text: Across eight published trials of AI negotiation coaching, sample sizes ranged from 48 to 612 participants, with a median of 180; only three were powered to detect effects smaller than 0.3 standard deviations.

The authors recruited 374 undergraduates and assigned them to AI-coached, human-coached, or no-coaching arms, noting that roughly 100 participants per cell were needed to detect the observed effect at 80% power.
The AI coaching intervention produced a medium effect size (Cohen's d=0.42) on objective deal outcomes relative to the no-coaching control.
Two raters coded the negotiation transcripts with high inter-rater reliability (Cohen's kappa = 0.81) before the analysis.
Each AI coaching session lasted on average 22 minutes per participant across the three conditions.
Recruiting professional negotiators is expensive, so most negotiation-coaching studies rely on student convenience samples.
An HNSW index gives sub-linear nearest-neighbour lookups over a vector column.

query: Do LLM negotiation agents concede too quickly compared to humans?

passages.text: When pitted against human counterparts, GPT-class negotiation agents conceded on price an average of 1.8 rounds earlier than humans and captured 9% less of the surplus, a gap the authors attribute to the model's bias toward agreeableness.
Ablating the politeness-tuned reward made the agent hold firm for more rounds before conceding and improved its captured value, directly linking premature concession to alignment tuning.
Human negotiators in the same protocol anchored aggressively and rarely made the first concession, whereas the baseline model frequently opened near its final offer.
LLM negotiation agents produced noticeably more polite and rapport-building messages than the human negotiators they were matched against.
Human negotiators took several seconds longer than the LLM agent to type each counter-offer in the chat interface.
pgvector adds an approximate nearest-neighbor index to Postgres via the HNSW method.

query: How is the novelty of an AI-generated research hypothesis evaluated?

passages.text: The authors operationalized novelty as the cosine distance between a generated hypothesis embedding and its nearest neighbor in an existing literature corpus, avoiding the unreliability of asking the model to rate its own originality.
Hypotheses were scored by expert reviewers on novelty, and the embedding-distance proxy correlated r=0.61 with the reviewers' novelty judgments.
Self-assessed novelty from the language model was systematically inflated, rating nearly every hypothesis as highly original regardless of overlap with prior work.
The model was also asked to rate the feasibility of each hypothesis on a five-point scale before any human review.
Generated hypotheses were scored for grammatical fluency by an automated language-quality metric.
SMTP over TLS on port 587 is the standard submission path for outbound email.

query: Does hybrid lexical-plus-dense retrieval improve recall of contradicting scientific claims?

passages.text: Adding a BM25 pass to dense retrieval raised recall of genuinely contradicting claim pairs from 0.71 to 0.88, because lexically overlapping but semantically reframed claims fell below the dense similarity threshold.
The union of dense and lexical candidate pairs surfaced contradictions that shared rare tokens such as dataset and metric names which the embedding model mapped far apart in vector space.
Dense-only retrieval favored paraphrase similarity and missed pairs that disagreed using different terminology for the same construct.
Adding the BM25 pass alongside dense retrieval increased per-query latency by about 40 milliseconds.
Dense retrieval alone achieved the highest precision on a clean paraphrase-detection benchmark with no distractors.
Fernet provides symmetric authenticated encryption built on AES-128 in CBC mode with an HMAC.

query: Does chain-of-thought prompting improve accuracy on multi-step arithmetic?

passages.text: Chain-of-thought prompting improved accuracy on multi-step arithmetic word problems by a large margin over direct-answer prompting on GSM8K.
Eliciting an explicit scratchpad of intermediate arithmetic steps roughly doubled exact-match accuracy on multi-digit addition and multiplication.
Chain-of-thought prompting also raised scores on commonsense reasoning benchmarks that involve no arithmetic at all.
On single-step arithmetic, chain-of-thought gave essentially no benefit and occasionally introduced spurious steps.
Chain-of-thought prompting increased the average generated output length by roughly three times.
RealDictCursor returns each database row as a Python dictionary keyed by column name.

query: Does self-consistency (sampling multiple chains and majority-voting) raise reasoning accuracy?

passages.text: Sampling multiple reasoning chains and taking the majority-vote answer (self-consistency) improved benchmark accuracy substantially over greedy single-chain decoding.
Majority voting over independently sampled reasoning paths raised accuracy on both arithmetic and commonsense benchmarks relative to a single greedy decode.
The accuracy gain from self-consistency grew with the diversity of the sampled reasoning paths.
Raising the sampling temperature degraded single-sample accuracy when no voting was applied.
Self-consistency increases inference cost roughly linearly with the number of samples drawn.
OpenAlex returns abstracts as an inverted index mapping each word to its positions.

query: Does retrieval-augmented generation reduce factual hallucination?

passages.text: Retrieval-augmented generation reduced factual hallucination on knowledge-intensive questions by grounding each answer in the retrieved passages rather than the model's parametric memory.
Augmenting the generator with retrieved evidence cut the rate of hallucinated entities on open-domain QA compared with a closed-book baseline.
When the retrieved context was irrelevant, retrieval augmentation introduced new errors as the model deferred to misleading passages.
Adding the retrieval step increased end-to-end answer latency because of the extra search round trip.
A RAG system needs a vector database to store and search the passage embeddings at query time.
bcrypt silently truncates passwords to 72 bytes, so hashing and verification must truncate consistently.

query: How does embedding dimensionality affect retrieval accuracy?

passages.text: Retrieval accuracy plateaued beyond 768 dimensions and degraded at very high dimensions as the added components captured noise rather than signal.
Across the tested corpora, increasing embedding dimensionality kept improving retrieval accuracy up to the largest model, with the 1536-dimension variant best.
Higher embedding dimensions increased the on-disk index storage size roughly linearly.
Quantizing the embeddings to int8 cut memory use sharply with only a small accuracy loss.
React escapes interpolated text by default, mitigating a class of cross-site scripting bugs.

query: Do cross-encoder rerankers produce more relevant rankings than bi-encoders?

passages.text: Cross-encoders outperformed bi-encoders on relevance because joint query-document attention captures term interactions a bi-encoder's independent embeddings cannot.
In-domain fine-tuned bi-encoders matched cross-encoder relevance on the target collection while retrieving an order of magnitude faster.
Cross-encoders are slower than bi-encoders at query time because they cannot precompute document representations offline.
Bi-encoders enable approximate nearest-neighbor search over precomputed document vectors.
The monitoring digest is delivered over Gmail SMTP using an app password.

query: Does practicing with a simulated AI conflict partner transfer to real unaided conflict performance?

passages.text: Participants who trained with the simulated conflict partner improved their measured conflict-resolution performance in a subsequent real, unaided conflict with a human confederate.
After simulated practice, trainees reduced their use of competitive strategies by 67% and doubled cooperative strategies in the real post-training conflict.
Simulated practice improved application of strategies in real interactions but did not improve strategy recognition or recall on a written test.
Participants rated the AI conflict simulation as realistic and engaging on a post-session survey.
The simulation's prompting pipeline classifies each utterance using an eight-strategy Interests-Rights-Power taxonomy.
psycopg2's ThreadedConnectionPool is safe to share across a thread pool.

query: Did the static handbook outperform the interactive AI coach on usability and empowerment?

passages.text: The static negotiation handbook significantly outperformed both AI conditions on usability (UMUX) and on psychological empowerment, with a medium effect size versus the interactive AI coach.
Participants preferred the static handbook over the interactive AI coach and rated it higher on perceived usefulness.
The interactive AI coach did win on one dimension, producing the largest reduction in negotiation-related fear among all conditions.
The interactive AI coach had a higher daily active-usage rate than the handbook during the study window.
The handbook was twelve pages long and took participants about fifteen minutes to read.
The Voyage rerank-2.5-lite model scores each query-passage pair jointly.

query: How did experienced humanitarian negotiators react to occasional LLM hallucinations?

passages.text: Experienced frontline humanitarian negotiators expressed tolerance for occasional LLM hallucinations, viewing AI errors as manageable given their own expertise to verify and triangulate.
Under time pressure, expert negotiators treated the model's occasional fabrications as a manageable cost, cross-checking AI output against their field knowledge.
Novice negotiators, by contrast, were at greater risk from the same hallucinations because they lacked the domain expertise to catch and correct AI errors.
The same negotiators preferred process-oriented AI tools that surfaced options and risks over tools that gave direct strategy recommendations.
The study interviewed eighteen humanitarian negotiators across six organizations.
APScheduler runs the daily monitor job in-process unless the standalone worker is used.

query: Does query expansion improve recall for short, underspecified queries?

passages.text: Query expansion with generated paraphrases improved Recall@20 for short, underspecified queries by six points over the unexpanded baseline.
Pseudo-relevance feedback raised recall most on short queries that lacked enough terms to match relevant documents directly.
Query expansion increased the average number of tokens sent to the retriever per request.
Long, detailed queries already achieved high recall and saw little additional benefit from expansion.
The corpus was deduplicated with MinHash LSH at a Jaccard threshold of 0.8 before indexing.

query: Is structured feedback necessary for people to improve at negotiation?

passages.text: People do not reliably learn negotiation tactics from the experience of bargaining alone; the study found structured feedback was necessary for measurable improvement.
Without explicit instruction, repeated negotiation practice produced no significant skill gain, whereas adding structured post-round feedback did.
A separate line of work reports that practice against autonomous agents improved negotiation ability even when no feedback was provided to the learner.
Participants rated the post-negotiation feedback as the most enjoyable part of the training program.
The feedback text in the system was generated by GPT-4o, chosen for its speed.
slowapi enforces per-IP rate limits on the expensive endpoints.

query: Does an SGLT2 inhibitor reduce all-cause mortality in heart failure?

passages.text: In a randomized trial of 3,730 heart-failure patients, the SGLT2 inhibitor reduced all-cause mortality by 17% (HR 0.83, 95% CI 0.71-0.97) over a median 18-month follow-up.
Pooled across two large trials, SGLT2 inhibition lowered the risk of death from any cause in patients with reduced ejection fraction.
The drug reduced cardiovascular death, a prespecified component of the primary composite endpoint.
The SGLT2 inhibitor significantly reduced heart-failure hospitalizations compared with placebo.
Treatment improved patients' Kansas City Cardiomyopathy Questionnaire quality-of-life scores at eight months.
In the diabetic subgroup the SGLT2 inhibitor lowered HbA1c by about 0.6 percentage points.
An HNSW index gives sub-linear nearest-neighbour lookups over a vector column.

query: Does mindfulness-based stress reduction lower clinical anxiety?

passages.text: An eight-week mindfulness-based stress reduction program reduced clinician-rated anxiety on the Hamilton scale versus a waitlist control, with a moderate effect size (d=0.55).
MBSR produced a clinically meaningful reduction in generalized-anxiety symptoms that was sustained at six-month follow-up.
Self-reported anxiety also fell after MBSR, though by less than the clinician-rated measure.
MBSR participants showed improved sustained attention on a continuous-performance task after the program.
Mindfulness training increased participants' self-reported life satisfaction.
Adherence to the daily home mindfulness practice averaged 4.2 of 7 days per week.
Fernet provides symmetric authenticated encryption built on AES-128-CBC with an HMAC.

query: Does large-scale reforestation provide durable long-term carbon sequestration?

passages.text: Across a 30-year chronosequence, reforested plots accumulated and retained both soil and biomass carbon, indicating durable long-term sequestration.
Process modeling showed the carbon stock of reforested stands remained stable for decades in the absence of major disturbance.
Wildfire can release much of the stored carbon from reforested stands within a single season, qualifying the durability of the sink.
Newly planted forests sequestered carbon most rapidly in their first five years before the rate slowed.
Reforestation sharply increased local biodiversity, including bird and pollinator species richness.
Reforestation on steep slopes reduced soil erosion and downstream sedimentation.
The pgvector <=> operator computes cosine distance between stored vectors.

query: How frequent are CRISPR-Cas9 off-target edits in human cells?

passages.text: Genome-wide sequencing detected off-target Cas9 edits at a median frequency of about 0.2% across twelve computationally predicted sites in human cells.
High-fidelity Cas9 variants lowered off-target editing to undetectable levels at most sites while preserving on-target activity.
On-target editing efficiency reached 85% at the intended locus in the same cell line.
Delivering Cas9 via lipid nanoparticles improved post-editing cell viability.
The guide RNA was designed with a standard 20-nucleotide spacer sequence.
React escapes interpolated text by default, mitigating cross-site scripting.

query: Does raising the minimum wage reduce employment?

passages.text: A difference-in-differences analysis across bordering counties found that minimum-wage increases had no statistically significant effect on employment in the affected sectors.
Other work reports small negative employment effects concentrated among the youngest and least-experienced workers.
The minimum-wage increase raised average earnings for low-wage workers by about 8%.
Higher minimum wages were associated with reduced employee turnover at affected firms.
Pass-through of the increase to consumer prices was modest, around 0.4% in full-service restaurants.

query: Does sleep after learning improve memory consolidation?

passages.text: Participants who slept after learning recalled significantly more word pairs than those who stayed awake for an equivalent interval, supporting sleep-dependent consolidation.
Slow-wave sleep duration predicted the overnight improvement on a declarative memory task.
Cueing recently learned material during slow-wave sleep (targeted memory reactivation) further boosted later retention.
Sleep deprivation impaired sustained attention and reaction time the following day.
A short daytime nap improved subjective alertness, though the study measured no memory outcome.
The proportion of REM sleep increased after participants withdrew from caffeine.
The Voyage rerank model scores each query-passage pair jointly.

query: Does spaced repetition improve long-term retention compared with massed practice?

passages.text: Items studied with spaced repetition were retained far better on a one-month delayed test than items studied through massed practice, a robust spacing effect.
Gradually expanding the inter-study interval produced higher delayed-test retention than fixed short intervals.
Students reported that massed cramming felt more effective, a metacognitive illusion not borne out by their test scores.
Spaced practice spread the same total study time across more, shorter sessions.
Flashcard apps that use spaced schedules reported higher daily active-user counts.

query: Have perovskite solar cells achieved long-term operational stability?

passages.text: Encapsulated perovskite cells retained 95% of their initial efficiency after 1,000 hours of continuous illumination, a marked stability improvement.
Compositional engineering extended the T80 operational lifetime to over 1,500 hours under damp-heat testing.
The perovskite cell reached a certified record power-conversion efficiency of 25.7%.
The perovskite absorber layer was deposited by a low-cost solution-processing route.
Perovskite absorbers show a broader absorption spectrum than crystalline silicon.

query: Does high dietary fiber intake reduce colorectal cancer risk?

passages.text: In a prospective cohort of 480,000 adults, the highest fiber-intake quintile had a 16% lower colorectal cancer incidence than the lowest.
A meta-analysis of cohort studies found each 10 g/day increase in fiber was associated with a 10% reduction in colorectal cancer risk.
In the same cohort, high fiber intake was associated with lower cardiovascular mortality.
Higher fiber intake correlated with better overall diet-quality scores.
Soluble fiber slowed gastric emptying in a small crossover trial.
The monitoring digest is delivered over Gmail SMTP with an app password.

query: Do larger touch targets reduce error rates on mobile interfaces?

passages.text: Increasing button size from 7mm to 10mm reduced the tap-error rate from 9% to 3% in a controlled mobile study.
Error rates fell monotonically with target size up to about 9mm, consistent with Fitts's law predictions.
Larger touch targets slightly increased task-completion time because of the longer finger travel distance.
Users rated the larger-button layout as more aesthetically pleasing.
The study recruited 40 participants who used their own personal smartphones.
